qa_pairs.question: What are some common options used in `sbatch` job scripts on Trillium to specify job requirements?
qa_pairs.answer: Common `sbatch` options include `--nodes` (number of nodes), `--ntasks-per-node` (number of tasks for srun/mpirun to launch per node), `--cpus-per-task` (number of cores per task, typically for OpenMP threads), `--time` (duration of the job), `--job-name` (to specify a name for the job), `--output` (file to redirect standard output to), and `--mail-type` (when to send email notifications). For GPU jobs, `--gpus-per-node` specifies the number of GPUs to use on each node.

qa_pairs.question: What are the permissible values for the `--gpus-per-node` option when submitting a job to the Trillium GPU subcluster?
qa_pairs.answer: On the Trillium GPU subcluster, the `--gpus-per-node` option only allows values of either 1 or 4 GPUs per node.

qa_pairs.question: How are memory requests handled for jobs submitted to Trillium using the `--mem` option?
qa_pairs.answer: Memory requests made with the `--mem` option are ignored on Trillium. Your CPU jobs always receive `N × 755 GiB` (or 768 GiB, given slight variation in descriptions) of RAM, where `N` is the number of nodes. GPU full-node jobs get the same amount of memory, while single-GPU jobs get `188 GiB`.
